comments:
Effect types, enums, and parameters for motion graphics.

Effects are visual filters and transformations applied to layers.
Each effect has parameters that can be animated.

## Architecture

Mirrors `Lattice.Effects` from the Haskell backend.

## Categories

- Blur/Sharpen: Gaussian, directional, radial blur
- Color Correction: Levels, curves, color balance
- Distort: Warp, displacement, mesh deform
- Generate: Fractal noise, gradients
- Stylize: Glow, shadows, scanlines
- Time: Echo, time displacement

declarations.comments: Type of parameter an effect can have.
declarations.title: EffectParameterType

declarations.comments: Type of animatable value for effects.
declarations.title: EffectAnimatableType

declarations.comments: Style of warp distortion (15 variants, matches Photoshop).
declarations.title: WarpStyle

declarations.comments: How glow is composited with source.
declarations.title: GlowCompositeMode

declarations.comments: Edge pinning mode for mesh deform.
declarations.title: PinningMode

declarations.comments: Blend mode for RGB split effect.
declarations.title: RGBSplitBlendMode

declarations.comments:
RGBA color for effect parameters.

RGB channels are 0-255, alpha is 0.0-1.0.
declarations.title: EffectRGBA

declarations.comments: Point on a curve (for bezier/spline parameters).
declarations.title: CurvePoint

declarations.comments: Instance of an effect parameter with current value.
declarations.title: EffectParameter

declarations.comments: Instance of an effect on a layer.
declarations.title: EffectInstance

declarations.comments: Effect instance with mesh deformation capabilities.
declarations.title: MeshDeformEffectInstance

name: Hydrogen.Schema.Motion.Effects
